about.title: About The Box
paragraphs: The Boombox has been Miami's underground refuge for six years, a place where artists experiment and audiences fall in love with what's next.

In the Bird Road Arts District, we mix music, art, and culture into one space. Electronic nights, live sessions, and exhibitions are examples of events that celebrate what Miami truly sounds and feels like.

social.title: Don't Miss Anything
social.subtitle: Follow us for upcoming events, releases, and more

hero.title: Full Circle
hero.subtitle: From warehouse kids to venue owners: The story of Miami's most authentic underground space

section_where_it_started.title: Where It Started
paragraphs: Long before The Boombox became Miami's underground sanctuary, it was just a forgotten warehouse park in western Miami-Dade. Not the glamorous parts of the city you see in magazines — this was the suburbs, the overlooked corners where kids grew up finding their own way.

"I remember my friends and I used to skip school all the time and go over to this little warehouse park to smoke weed, do graffiti — all our little activities," recalls one of the founders. "It was our spot. Away from everything, just us and the walls."

The area wasn't much to look at — concrete, chain-link fences, spray paint layered on top of spray paint. But for a group of restless teenagers in the early 2010s, it was freedom. No rules, no authority, just raw creative expression and the kind of bonding that happens when you're young and finding your identity in the margins.

pull_quote_1.text: I think it's funny that we're throwing these huge parties there now, making a name for ourselves. It all came full circle.
pull_quote_1.author: The Boombox Founders

section_bird_road.title: The Bird Road Arts District
section_bird_road.image_alt: Bird Road Arts District warehouse exterior during daytime
paragraphs: What most people don't know is that this area — the Bird Road Arts District — was Miami's original underground arts hub, long before Wynwood became the city's commercialized art epicenter.

Back in the 1970s, the landlord's father bought the land for next to nothing. It was industrial, unglamorous, real. The neighborhood was a patchwork of carpentry shops, gardening supply stores, mechanic garages — working-class Miami at its core. Artists gravitated here because rent was cheap and nobody was watching.

By the time the founders were teenagers in the 2010s, the district had faded from public memory. The artists had moved on, the storefronts were weathered, and the warehouses sat quiet. But the energy was still there, embedded in the walls, waiting for someone to bring it back to life.
This wasn't prime real estate. It was overlooked, forgotten, raw — and that's exactly what made it perfect.

section_the_vision.title: The Vision
section_the_vision.image_alt: The Boombox venue interior with crowd at event
paragraphs: Years later, those same kids who used to skip school and paint the walls had grown up. They'd traveled, experienced different music scenes, but always felt something was missing in Miami's nightlife.

The city had clubs, sure — but they were corporate, overpriced, pretentious. The underground was either too sketchy or didn't exist at all. Western Miami-Dade, where they grew up, was a cultural desert for anyone who wanted authentic, cutting-edge music experiences.

So they decided to create what they always wanted: a space that felt like home. Somewhere that honored the underground but stayed legal and safe. A venue that served the community they came from — locals, artists, outsiders, misfits. People who just wanted real music, real vibes, no bullshit.
In 2018, The Boombox opened its doors in the exact same warehouse park where they used to hang out as teenagers. The full circle wasn't planned — it just happened.

pull_quote_2.text: People still do graffiti there. Our spot has gotten hit a couple of times. It's cool to see there are still kids on a mission.
pull_quote_2.author: The Boombox Founders

section_today.title: Today
section_today.image_alt: The Boombox today - Miami's underground music venue
paragraphs: Six years later, The Boombox has become what it was always meant to be: Miami's underground sanctuary. A place where emerging artists find their voice and music lovers discover their next obsession.
The walls still get tagged. The neighborhood hasn't changed much. The venue is still in the same unglamorous part of town. And that's the point.

While other venues chase trends and instagram aesthetics, The Boombox stays rooted in what it's always been: authentic, local, for the people who get it. The same kids who grew up feeling like outsiders now have a home. The same community that raised the founders now has a space to call their own.
From skipping school to running the show. From spray paint on walls to hosting international artists. From forgotten warehouse to cultural landmark.

cta.title: Experience The Boombox Yourself
cta.subtitle: See what 6 years of authentic underground culture looks like.

hero.title: What We Do
hero.subtitle: From coffee Sundays to underground raves, The Boombox is Miami's premier destination for culture, community, and unforgettable experiences.

activities.title: Coffee & Mart
activities.vibe: Tres leches lattes & hour-long lines worth the wait
activities.description: Experience Miami's hottest coffee trailer serving unique sugary lattes with flavors like Lucky Charms, french toast, and s'mores. Perfect for those who love varying levels of sugar and milk in their coffee.

activities.title: Art Club
activities.description: Bi-weekly creative sessions where local artists and art enthusiasts gather to create, collaborate, and connect. From painting to mixed media, all skill levels welcome.

activities.title: Car Shows
activities.description: Monthly automotive meetups featuring everything from classics to customs, exotics to daily drivers. Music, awards, and a community of car enthusiasts.

activities.title: Electronic Nights
activities.description: Miami's hottest electronic music events featuring top DJs and producers. From house to techno, experience cutting-edge sound in an intimate venue.

activities.title: Live Music
activities.description: Live performances across genres - from indie rock to Latin jazz. Experience music in an intimate setting with world-class acoustics.

hero.title: Work With Us
hero.subtitle_2: We build partnerships with our community
hero.body: Join promoters, artists, vendors, and creators who share our values of authenticity, creativity, and inclusivity

philosophy.title: More than a venue
philosophy.body: We work with promoters, artists, vendors, and creators who share our values of authenticity, creativity, and inclusivity. Every collaboration is a partnership — we invest in your success because your success is ours.

collaborate.opportunities_title: Three Ways to Work With Us

opportunities.title: Event Collaborations
opportunities.subtitle: Bring Your Vision to Life
opportunities.description: Have an event concept? Let's make it happen together. We partner with promoters, collectives, and artists to host unforgettable nights—from underground raves to live shows, genre-fluid parties to themed experiences.
how_it_works: We discuss your vision and audience
Collaborate on talent, production, and promotion
Split door sales and bar revenue (terms negotiated)
You bring the vibe, we bring the space and infrastructure
perfect_for: DJs and artists building their brand
Genre-specific nights (electronic, hip-hop, indie, emo, etc.)

opportunities.title: Vendor & Community Events
opportunities.subtitle: Join Our Market Days
opportunities.description: Calling all vendors, artisans, and small businesses! The Boombox regularly hosts flea markets, car shows, coffee Sundays, and community gatherings. We're always looking for quality vendors to participate and showcase their goods.
event_types: Monthly flea markets and pop-up shops
additional_info: Booth fees and details shared upon approval

opportunities.title: Rehearsal Space Rental
opportunities.subtitle: Perfect Your Sound
opportunities.description: Need a space to rehearse, record, or create? The Boombox offers our venue as a rehearsal space for bands, musicians, and performers during off-hours. Full sound system, stage setup, and room to work on your craft.
what_you_get: 200-person capacity space (great for full band setup)

items.question: Do you rent the venue for private events like birthdays or weddings?
items.answer: We focus on music and community events rather than private celebrations. However, if you have a creative concept that aligns with our vibe, reach out and let's talk!

items.question: What's the difference between "renting" and "collaborating"?
items.answer: We don't do traditional venue rentals where you pay a flat fee. Instead, we collaborate on events—sharing responsibilities, costs, and revenue. This keeps us invested in your success and maintains our community-first approach.

items.question: How far in advance should I reach out?
items.answer: For event collaborations: 4-8 weeks minimum. For rehearsal space: 1-2 weeks. For vendor spots: ASAP as spaces fill quickly.

items.question: What's your capacity?
items.answer: 200 people. It's intimate, which creates an incredible energy.

items.question: Is parking really free?
items.answer: Yes! Abundant free parking—one of our biggest advantages over downtown/South Beach venues.

items.question: What genres/styles do you prioritize?
items.answer: We're genre-agnostic! Electronic, hip-hop, indie, emo, punk, live bands—if it's authentic and brings community together, we're interested.

final_cta.title: Ready to Work Together?
final_cta.body: Whether you're planning an event, need rehearsal space, or want to join our vendor community—we'd love to hear from you.
